title: update build.py as to use env prioritized
body: Developers would prefer to build the src locally thus taking their own `.env` (if not exists then use env.example) to build the final `CursorPro` executable. Do you like it @chengazhen ?

step_plan.summary: Update `build.py` so local builds copy a developer’s `.env` into the dist output when present, falling back to `.env.example` only when `.env` is missing, with clearer messaging for missing files.

steps.operation: Implement env-file selection and copy logic that prefers local .env over .env.example (with clear logging and a helpful error if neither exists), while preserving existing platform-specific copy mechanics and output layout.
steps.reason: Current build always copies `.env.example` into the dist output as `.env`, overriding/ignoring a developer’s local `.env`. Updating `build()` to select `.env` first (fallback to `.env.example`) meets the requested build-time behavior and aligns with runtime expectations that a `.env` exists next to the executable.
steps.side_effects: Build output will change for developers who have a local `.env` (their values will be embedded into `dist/<platform>/.env`). If neither `.env` nor `.env.example` exists, the build may now fail fast (or emit a stronger warning) depending on the chosen behavior; this can surface missing config earlier. Any CI/release pipeline that relied on `.env.example` always being copied should be checked; logs will now indicate which file was used.
